title: Fig. 3 in Eco-epidemiological screening of multi-host wild rodent communities in the UK reveals pathogen strains of zoonotic interest
author: Occhibove, Flavia
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Fig. 3. Bayesian phylogenetic tree of 18S ribosomal RNA sequences of Babesia microti isolates, indicating the position of the Munich strain-like isolate obtained from the tick Ixodes trianguliceps from a bank vole in Ceredigion, Wales. Sequences of the cogeneric species B. vulpes and B. rodhaini are used as outgroups.
steps_raw: Fig. 3. Bayesian phylogenetic tree of 18S ribosomal RNA sequences of Babesia microti isolates, indicating the position of the Munich strain-like isolate obtained from the tick Ixodes trianguliceps from a bank vole in Ceredigion, Wales. Sequences of the cogeneric species B. vulpes and B. rodhaini are used as outgroups.
